title: westringia discipulorum
intro: Westringia discipulorum, also known as the Western Native Blueflower, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Westringia discipulorum include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Westringia discipulorum are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
